cards.title: 2028 Republican presidential nominee
cards.headline: J.D. Vance Is Underpriced for the 2028 GOP Nomination
cards.summary: Vance's 37.5% market price significantly underestimates the historical dominance of sitting Vice Presidents in primaries, his commanding 42% polling lead, and his lock on party infrastructure. The best opportunities are buying Vance and selling overpriced longshots like Tucker Carlson and Donald Trump Sr.
why: Vice Presidents who seek their party's nomination have historically won ~90% of the time, and Vance holds a 28-point polling lead over his nearest rival
Tucker Carlson at 7.65% is wildly overpriced after publicly breaking with Trump and lacking any campaign infrastructure
Donald Trump Sr. at 3.55% is essentially free money to sell — the 22nd Amendment bars him from a third term
cards.watch_item: A significant escalation of U.S. military involvement in Iran could crater Vance's approval and open the door to a serious challenger like Rubio or DeSantis

cards.title: Strait of Hormuz traffic returns to normal by end of April?
cards.headline: Strait of Hormuz Traffic Recovery by April 30 Is Virtually Impossible
cards.summary: With the Strait effectively closed at 0–3 ships per day, an active IRGC blockade, and only 10 days remaining, reaching the required 7-day average of ~60 daily transits is arithmetically near-impossible — making this market dramatically overpriced at ~29%.
why: Traffic has collapsed over 95% from pre-war levels (~130/day to 0–3/day), and Iran's IRGC re-closed the strait on April 18, firing on vessels
Resolution requires ~420 cumulative transits over 7 consecutive days — a pace that cannot be reached from a standing start even with an instant ceasefire
Insurance re-rating, vessel repositioning, and shipper confidence restoration historically take weeks to months, far exceeding the remaining window
cards.watch_item: Any surprise comprehensive peace deal or unexpected data methodology quirk in IMF Portwatch that could redefine how transits are counted

cards.title: Who will Trump pardon?
cards.headline: Pete Rose Can't Be Pardoned — He's Dead
cards.summary: The market prices a 23.5% chance Trump pardons Pete Rose, but Rose died on September 30, 2024, making a presidential pardon legally impossible. This represents the clearest mispricing across all 49 pardon contracts.
why: Pete Rose passed away in September 2024 — you cannot pardon a deceased person
The market still prices this at 23.5% despite a near-zero fair probability
This offers roughly 21.5 percentage points of edge with high confidence
cards.watch_item: Any unprecedented symbolic or posthumous gesture by Trump that the market's resolution criteria might count as a 'pardon' — extremely unlikely but worth monitoring

cards.title: Will Trump be impeached and removed from office?
cards.headline: Trump Removal Market Vastly Overpriced at 26.5%
cards.summary: No U.S. president has ever been removed via impeachment, and the structural barriers — especially the 67-vote Senate threshold — make this outcome extremely unlikely even over a 2.7-year horizon. Fair probability sits around 6%, creating a substantial edge for NO.
why: Zero presidents removed by impeachment in 235 years of U.S. history (0 for 46)
Senate conviction requires ~17 Republican defections — far beyond the 7 who crossed over in 2021
Democrats are internally divided on impeachment and may not even control the House until 2027
cards.watch_item: A major, unprecedented crisis — such as a nuclear incident or massive scandal — that fractures the Republican Senate coalition beyond anything seen in modern politics

cards.title: Will Elon win his case against OpenAI?
cards.headline: Musk v. OpenAI: Market Overprices Musk's Chances After Dropping 24 of 26 Claims
cards.summary: With Musk narrowing his case to just two claims — breach of charitable trust and unjust enrichment — and facing a significant donor-standing legal hurdle under California law, the market's ~58% implied probability of a Musk win appears inflated against a fair value estimate near 38%.
why: California law typically reserves charitable trust enforcement to the Attorney General, not private donors — Musk's $44M was a tax-deductible gift, not an investment
Dropping 24 of 26 claims on the eve of trial, including all fraud charges, signals significant evidentiary weakness
Structural asymmetry: hung jury, mistrial, settlement without an explicit Musk victory, or delayed ruling all resolve as a loss for YES bettors
cards.watch_item: Whether Judge Gonzalez Rogers recognizes a novel donor-standing theory for charitable trust claims — this single legal question could flip the entire case

cards.title: US x Iran permanent peace deal by April 22, 2026?
cards.headline: US-Iran Permanent Peace Deal by April 22 Is Near-Impossible
cards.summary: With fewer than 24 hours until the deadline, talks haven't even begun, Iran hasn't confirmed attendance, and the resolution requires a formal permanent peace deal—not a framework, ceasefire, or memorandum of understanding. The market at ~19.5% dramatically overprices YES.
why: Resolution demands a PERMANENT deal—an impossibly high bar when prior rounds collapsed and parties remain 'worlds apart' on nuclear issues
Iran hasn't confirmed participation in Islamabad talks; its IRGC is pressuring negotiators to refuse under the current blockade
Trump's repeated claims of imminent deals have already proven unreliable, and current draft proposals focus on explicitly temporary measures
cards.watch_item: A surprise joint announcement or ambiguous declaration by both governments that could be interpreted as meeting the 'permanent deal' resolution criteria

cards.title: Who will leave Trump's Cabinet next?
cards.headline: Tulsi Gabbard Most Likely Next Cabinet Departure — Lutnick Contract Looks Overpriced
cards.summary: Tulsi Gabbard is the clear frontrunner to be the next Trump Cabinet member out, trading at 55% but worth closer to 60%. Meanwhile, Howard Lutnick's 18.5% contract appears significantly overpriced given his central role in tariff policy and absence of departure signals.
why: Gabbard has been publicly sidelined — recent reports highlight her marginalization and friction over FISA 702 surveillance policy
The market's total probabilities sum to ~155%, meaning long-shot contracts are systematically overpriced by construction
Lutnick remains operationally critical as Trump's point man on tariffs with no credible exit rumors
cards.watch_item: Whether Gabbard's sidelining leads to a formal resignation or if she quietly stays on indefinitely, which would erode the time advantage priced into her contract

cards.title: 2028 Democratic presidential nominee
cards.headline: Kamala Harris Is Dramatically Underpriced for 2028 Democratic Nomination
cards.summary: Harris polls at 22-24% in early Democratic primary surveys but trades at just 6.4% on Kalshi — the largest polling-to-price gap in the market. Even after discounting for the possibility she doesn't run, fair value sits around 14%, more than double the current price.
why: Harris leads national Democratic primary polls at 22-24% while trading at only 6.4% — a massive disconnect
She is actively signaling a 2028 run with policy speeches and party positioning, plus retains unmatched name ID and donor infrastructure
Other candidates like Ossoff (8.6% with zero polling support) appear overpriced, suggesting broad market mispricing
cards.watch_item: Whether Harris makes a definitive decision not to run — that would collapse her value instantly and is the single biggest risk to this bet

cards.title: Will Blue Origin land on the moon before SpaceX?
cards.headline: Blue Origin Moon Race Odds Look Slightly Overpriced
cards.summary: The market prices Blue Origin landing on the moon before SpaceX at 66.5%, but a fair estimate is closer to 60% — the substantial chance that neither company lands before 2030 tilts the odds toward NO more than the market reflects.
why: New Glenn is grounded after its second upper-stage failure in just 3 flights, raising systemic reliability concerns for Blue Origin's lunar ambitions
A 15-25% probability that neither company lands before the 2030 deadline is a major NO component that race-framing tends to underweight
SpaceX's far higher flight cadence (12 Starship flights vs. 3 total for Blue Origin) gives it a faster problem-solving loop despite harder orbital refueling requirements
cards.watch_item: New Glenn's return-to-flight timeline — a quick fix to the upper-stage issue would significantly strengthen the case for Blue Origin landing first

cards.title: Will OpenAI or Anthropic IPO first?
cards.headline: Anthropic Has the IPO Momentum, but Neither Side Offers a Strong Bet
cards.summary: Anthropic is actively eyeing an October 2026 IPO with a $1T valuation and clean governance, while OpenAI faces revenue misses and CFO reservations about timing — but a 14-year resolution window makes current signals unreliable enough to warrant staying on the sidelines.
why: Anthropic is reportedly weighing an October 2026 IPO and rejected an $800B pre-IPO round, signaling readiness to go public first
OpenAI missed revenue and user targets, with analysts saying it likely won't go public this year despite massive capital needs
Both contracts are close to fair value after accounting for the ~7% market vig, leaving no clear edge on either side
cards.watch_item: Any announcement that OpenAI has formally filed or accelerated IPO plans due to its $600B spending commitments would dramatically shift the calculus

cards.title: When will any company achieve AGI? · Before Jan 1, 2027
cards.headline: Formal AGI Declaration Before 2027 Remains Unlikely — Market Overpriced
cards.summary: No company has made or signaled intent to make an official AGI announcement, and strong disincentives make one unlikely before January 1, 2027. The market at 14.5% overprices this outcome relative to a fair estimate near 9%.
why: Resolution requires an explicit official corporate announcement using the term 'AGI' — a bar no company has ever met
Major labs face regulatory, liability, and reputational risks from declaring AGI, creating strong disincentives
Industry trend is shifting toward practical AI applications, not moonshot AGI claims — even OpenAI removed its AGI clause from its Microsoft deal
cards.watch_item: Any formal press release or official corporate statement from a frontier AI lab explicitly claiming AGI achievement — particularly from unpredictable actors like Elon Musk's xAI

cards.title: Kharg Island no longer under Iranian control by April 30?
cards.headline: Kharg Island Stays Iranian — Market Overprices an Implausible Scenario
cards.summary: With only 10 days remaining, Iran maintains full operational control of Kharg Island, with tankers actively loading oil as of April 17 and no credible military operation underway to seize the territory. The market at ~8% significantly overprices what is realistically a ~3% tail-risk event.
why: Iranian tankers are actively loading oil at Kharg Island right now — clear, unambiguous operational control
No amphibious invasion is underway or credibly planned, and even one launched today couldn't establish territorial control in 10 days
The resolution requires another force to establish actual governmental or military control — an extraordinarily high bar that no actor is even attempting
cards.watch_item: Any indication of a US or allied amphibious military operation targeting Kharg Island, though no current intelligence or reporting suggests this is remotely on the table

cards.title: 2028 U.S. Presidential Election winner?
cards.headline: Marco Rubio Overpriced at 15.5% for 2028 Presidential Win
cards.summary: Rubio's 15.5% market price significantly overstates his realistic path to the presidency, with actual primary polls showing him at just 14% compared to J.D. Vance's commanding 42% among GOP voters. Selling Rubio offers roughly 4.5% edge based on the compound probability of winning both a contested primary and the general election.
why: Rubio trails Vance badly in real primary polls (14% vs 42%), despite a misleading CPAC straw poll surge that historically has little predictive value
J.D. Vance holds massive structural advantages as sitting Vice President, making Rubio's path to the GOP nomination steep and narrow
A fair compound probability of ~11% for Rubio to win both the nomination and the general election leaves meaningful downside in the current 15.5% price
cards.watch_item: Vance's historically low approval ratings (37%) — if they collapse further or Iran-related policy backlash lands squarely on the VP, Rubio could emerge as the credible GOP alternative and close the polling gap

cards.title: Who will be confirmed as Fed chair?
cards.headline: Kevin Warsh Remains Overwhelming Favorite for Fed Chair — But Market Leaves No Room to Trade
cards.summary: Warsh is the only formal nominee, has completed his confirmation hearing, and has roughly 2.7 years of runway to clear the Senate. At 95¢, the market is pricing him slightly rich versus a ~92% fair probability, but the thin edge and long capital lockup make trading unattractive.
why: Sen. Tillis is blocking the committee vote until a DOJ probe of Powell is dropped, but the hold is tactical — Tillis says he 'likes Warsh' and is retiring in January
No alternative candidate is formally nominated, and all three listed alternatives (Shelton, Rieder, Hassett) trade at trivial penny-level prices with negligible edge
With the market closing January 1, 2029, the extended timeline heavily favors eventual confirmation even if the current standoff drags on
cards.watch_item: Whether Trump agrees to drop the DOJ investigation into former Chair Powell — resolving this would likely clear Tillis's blockade and push Warsh's confirmation probability toward near-certainty
